output: Implemented T5. I rewrote `useJoinClipsSettings` coverage around the public hook contract and verified it with the full focused module plus `npx tsc --noEmit`. While making those behavior tests pass, I fixed two adapter reconciliation bugs in `useJoinClipsSettings`: an infinite resync loop on authoritative reconciliation, and a race where an immediate authoritative read could be clobbered by the lazy store bootstrap before revert/reset semantics stabilized. A checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_4.json`.
commands_run: timeout 20s npx vitest run --reporter=verbose --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts; echo EXIT:$?
deviations: `T5` required a small production fix in `src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts` because the rewritten public-behavior tests exposed two real adapter regressions: a repeated authoritative-sync loop and a lazy-load clobber race. I kept the fix local to the Join Clips adapter and did not broaden scope beyond supporting the tested public contract.
One intermediate edit was applied through `exec_command` before the user warning. I stopped doing that immediately afterward and used the dedicated `apply_patch` tool for subsequent file edits.

task_updates.executor_notes: Rewrote the Join Clips hook tests around the public API instead of the legacy `useAutoSaveSettings` delegation. The suite now covers nullish-ID inert behavior and real-ID transitions, loading-to-ready reconciliation timing, debounced persistence, `initializeFrom` staying a no-op, `revert`/`reset(newDefaults?)` clean semantics, `saveImmediate(dataToSave?)` cache sync plus invalidation, and lifecycle flushes on project change, `beforeunload`, and unmount. While making those tests pass, I fixed two adapter reconciliation bugs in `useJoinClipsSettings`: repeated authoritative syncs could recurse indefinitely, and an immediate authoritative read could be clobbered by the lazy store load before reset/revert semantics were stable. Verified with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The rewritten Join Clips hook tests now validate behavior strictly through the exported hook contract: disabled/nullish sentinel safety, real-project loading timing until authoritative reconciliation, `initializeFrom` remaining a no-op, `reset`/`revert` leaving the hook clean, `saveImmediate` cache sync plus invalidation, and lifecycle flushes on project change, `beforeunload`, and unmount. The focused suite passes after the adapter-local reconciliation fixes.
